scene_input.scene_summary: Jubayr and al-Ḥārith receive news of Saʿd's torture.
scene_input.key_visual_action: Jubayr and al-Ḥārith shocked, looking at each other in concern

schema_output.scene_summary: Jubayr and al-Ḥārith receive news of Saʿd's torture.
schema_output.source_reference: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, p. 114
schema_output.moral_lesson: Importance of compassion and empathy in the face of adversity

characters.name: Jubayr ibn Muṭʿim
characters.appearance: medium build, young adult, wearing a simple white thobe with a woolen cloak draped over the shoulders

characters.name: al-Ḥārith ibn Ḥarb
characters.appearance: slender build, young adult, wearing a simple off-white thobe with a plain woolen cloak

schema_output.narrative_text: Jubayr and al-Ḥārith were deeply concerned when they heard the news of Saʿd's torture. They looked at each other in shock, unsure of what to do.
schema_output.image_prompt: Children's watercolor storybook illustration, soft warm hand-painted style for ages 6-12. A desolate desert landscape or a bustling marketplace in a 7th century Arabian setting with sandy dunes or sandy-colored buildings in the background. Late afternoon sunlight casts a warm golden glow across the scene. In the foreground, two young men sit facing each other, their faces seen from behind. On the left, the central figure in a simple white thobe with a woolen cloak draped over the shoulders, one hand raised in a gesture of concern, his head turned slightly towards the man on the right. On the right, the man in a simple off-white thobe with a plain woolen cloak, his head bowed in shock, one hand covering his mouth in distress. Soft warm golden light, concerned and somber mood. Medium close shot at eye level. Warm beige, sandy, and golden palette. No modern objects, historically accurate 7th century scene.
_safety_changes: 'young men' -> 'two young men', 'faces obscured from view' -> 'faces seen from behind', 'Jubayr ibn Muṭʿim' -> 'the central figure', 'al-Ḥārith ibn Ḥarb' -> 'the man on the right'
